methods.description: This method involves the identification and analysis of pollen types from samples collected over a 14-week period. The analysis aims to understand the deposition patterns and seasonal variations of airborne pollen.

steps.description: A total of 54 pollen types were identified from samples taken through the period of 14 weeks.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and catalog pollen types from collected samples over 14 weeks

steps.description: Only 19 pollen types were included in the data matrix and displayed in the pollen diagram, which were represented by values higher than 2% or more.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter and include pollen types with values higher than 2% in data matrix

steps.description: The pollen diagram shows that deposition of airborne pollen took place throughout the entire period of 14 weeks.
steps.searchable_summary: Create pollen deposition diagram for 14-week period

steps.description: The dendrogram at the end of the pollen diagram shows the groups obtained by cluster analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform cluster analysis to group pollen types and create dendrogram

steps.description: Two main groups A and B were differentiated. Abundant taxa in group A showed a continuous pattern throughout the entire period or from the first to sixth week of observation (early). In contrast, taxa of group B, represented by H. annus, showed a marked seasonality between seventh and fourteenth week.
steps.searchable_summary: Differentiate and analyze patterns in pollen groups A and B

steps.description: Within these groups different periods were differentiated by subgroups as high values of Poaceae 25 µm in the fifth and sixth weeks.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and analyze subgroups within pollen groups based on period differentiation
